dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4083 IS: Working Americans’ Tax Cut Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Van Hollen (for himself, Mr. Kelly, Mrs. Gillibrand, Mr. Booker, Mr. Kim, Mr. Durbin, Mr. Sanders, Mr. Merkley, Mr. Coons, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Schatz, Ms. Baldwin, Ms. Hirono, Mr. Heinrich, Mr. King, Mr. Markey, Mr. Welch, Mr. Schiff, and Ms. Blunt Rochester) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to make certain modifications in relation to the taxation of income required to fund basic living expenses, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Working Americans’ Tax Cut Act.

section.header: Alternative maximum tax for low-income individuals

subsection.text: Part I of subchapter A of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting after section 1 the following new section:

section.header: Alternative maximum tax for low- and middle-income individuals

subsection.text: In the case of a qualified individual, the tax imposed under section 1 for any taxable year shall not exceed 25.5 percent of the excess of—

paragraph.text: the taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income for such taxable year, over
the cost-of-living exemption for such taxable year.

For purposes of this section, the term qualified individual means, with respect to any taxable year, any individual if the taxpayer's modified adjusted gross income for such taxable year is less than 175 percent of the cost-of-living exemption for such taxable year.
The term qualified individual shall not include any person described in section 63(c)(6).
The term cost-of-living exemption means, with respect to any taxable year—

subparagraph.text: in the case of a taxpayer not described in subparagraph (B) or (C), 100 percent of the annualized cost-of-living wage,
in the case of a joint return, 200 percent of the annualized cost-of-living wage, and
in the case of a head of household, 140 percent of the annualized cost-of-living wage.
The term annualized cost-of-living wage means, with respect to any taxable year, an amount equal to $46,000, multiplied by the ratio of—

clause.text: the CPI–U for the calendar year preceding the calendar year in which such taxable year begins, to
the CPI–U for the calendar year preceding the calendar year of the date of enactment of the Working Americans’ Tax Cut Act.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term CPI–U means, when used with respect to a calendar year, the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers, as published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, for September of such year.

subsection.text: For purposes of this section, the term modified adjusted gross income means adjusted gross income increased by—

paragraph.text: any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933, and
an amount equal to the portion of the taxpayer's social security benefits (as defined in section 86(d)) which is not included in gross income under section 86 for the taxable year.

subsection.text: The table of sections for part I of subchapter A of chapter 1 of such Code is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 1 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 1A. Alternative maximum tax for low- and middle-income individuals.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
Subchapter A of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new part:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 59B. Surcharge on high income individuals.

subsection.text: In the case of a taxpayer other than a corporation, there is hereby imposed (in addition to any other tax imposed by this subtitle) a tax equal to the sum of—

paragraph.text: 5 percent of so much of the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer as exceeds $1,000,000, but does not exceed $2,000,000,
10 percent of so much of the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer as exceeds $2,000,000, but does not exceed $5,000,000, plus
12 percent of so much of the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer as exceeds $5,000,000.
In the case of any taxable year beginning after 2026, subsection (a) shall be applied by substituting each dollar amount in such subsection with an amount equal to the product of—

subparagraph.text: such dollar amount (as determined without regard to this subsection), multiplied by
an amount equal to the ratio of—

clause.text: the CPI–U for the calendar year preceding the calendar year in which such taxable year begins, to
the CPI–U for the calendar year preceding the calendar year of the date of enactment of the Working Americans’ Tax Cut Act.

paragraph.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term CPI–U means, when used with respect to a calendar year, the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers, as published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, for September of such year.

subsection.text: In the case of any taxpayer filing a joint return under section 6013, subsection (a) shall be applied (after the application of subsection (b)) by increasing each of the dollar amounts by an amount equal to 50 percent of such dollar amount.

For purposes of this section, the term modified adjusted gross income means adjusted gross income reduced by any deduction (not taken into account in determining adjusted gross income) allowed for investment interest (as defined in section 163(d)). In the case of an estate or trust, adjusted gross income shall be determined as provided in section 67(e).

paragraph.text: The dollar amount in effect under subsection (a) (after the application of subsections (b) and (c)) shall be decreased by the excess of—

subparagraph.text: the amounts excluded from the taxpayer’s gross income under section 911, over
the amounts of any deductions or exclusions disallowed under section 911(d)(6) with respect to the amounts described in subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a trust all the unexpired interests in which are devoted to one or more of the purposes described in section 170(c)(2)(B).

paragraph.header: Not treated as tax imposed by this chapter for certain purposes
paragraph.text: The tax imposed under this section shall not be treated as tax imposed by this chapter for purposes of determining the amount of any credit under this chapter or for purposes of section 55.

subsection.text: The table of parts for subchapter A of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Part VIII—Surcharge on high income individuals

subsection.text: The amendment made by subsection (a) shall not be treated as a change in a rate of tax for purposes of section 15 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
